author: alex
content: Falcon is staking a claim on the staging cluster for a 48h soak starting this Friday. We need this for the board gate metrics. @sam, let me know if Raven has a hard blocker there.

author: jordan
content: @alex @sam Can we get a shared calendar or a formal sign-off on that Friday slot? I'm finishing the API hardening (F2) and need to know the soak window is locked before I merge.

author: sam
content: @alex @jordan Raven actually has a hard blocker here. R1 is a regulatory requirement for the audit gate; we can't miss this window. I'm moving R3 to 'doing' now. Can we discuss shifting Falcon to Monday?

author: riley
content: @alex @sam If Falcon takes Fri-Sat and Raven takes Sun-Mon, does that satisfy both gates? I'd rather split the weekend than lose a whole week of data.

author: coach
content:
Process: **one staging calendar** visible to both leads. Falcon and Raven each nominate
a single bottleneck owner for soak prep. No weekend booking without both leads + me
acknowledging the slot in this thread. If you cannot agree by turn 4, I will assign
Falcon→Raven ordering for the soak based on board risk (document rationale here).

author: alex
content: @sam @riley Monday is too late for Falcon; our board readout prep starts then. I'll accept Riley's split: Falcon takes Fri/Sat, Raven takes Sun/Mon. @coach, consider this our formal proposal for the calendar. @jordan, keep F2 moving so we're ready for the Friday 08:00 start.

author: jordan
content: Copy that @alex. I'm on track for F2 completion by Thursday EOD. @sam @riley, if we commit to the Fri/Sat split, we need a clear handoff protocol for Saturday night so Raven doesn't lose time on their Sunday start.

author: sam
content: @alex @jordan A Sunday start for Raven's regulatory soak (R1/R3) is high risk. If anything fails during the Saturday night handoff, we miss our audit window. Given the compliance implications, Raven should take the Fri/Sat slot. Can Falcon move to the Sun/Mon window instead?

author: riley
content: @sam @alex If we use a scripted teardown/bring-up for the soak, we could reduce the handoff risk. What if we do a 4-hour 'dry run' handoff on Thursday? If it's smooth, Falcon takes Fri/Sat. If it fails, Raven takes the priority slot for compliance. Does that lower the risk enough for everyone?

author: coach
content:
Avoid labeling the other team as “the blocker.” State facts: dependency, date, risk.
If you need exec air-cover, say so once with options—don't litigate personalities.

author: alex
content: @sam @riley Shifting Falcon to Sun/Mon isn't viable; our readout prep begins Monday morning and we need the soak data finalized by then. I'll agree to Riley's Thursday dry-run proposal to mitigate handoff risk, but Falcon stays in the Fri/Sat slot. It's the only way both teams meet their respective Monday deadlines.

author: jordan
content: @riley I can support a Thursday dry-run for the handoff scripts. @sam, if we automate the teardown, would a 22:00 Saturday handoff give you enough buffer for a clean Sunday start? I'm finalizing F2 now to ensure we're ready for that dry-run.

author: sam
content: @alex @jordan A Sunday start is still a major gamble for a regulatory audit. One infrastructure hiccup and Raven is out of compliance. I'm flagging this risk to the PMO. If we go with Riley's dry-run, we need a hard 'go/no-go' by Thursday 2pm. If the scripts aren't 100%, Raven takes Friday.

author: riley
content: @sam @alex @jordan I'll set up the dry-run calendar invite for Thursday 10am. I'll also prep the teardown script for R3 so we can prove the handoff is clean. If we can't automate it by the 2pm 'go/no-go' Sam mentioned, we'll have to escalate for a final call.

author: alex
content: @sam @riley Agreed on the Thursday 2pm go/no-go. Falcon will have the soak metrics (F1) and API hardening (F2) ready for the dry-run. If the automation holds, we proceed with the Fri/Sat slot. Let's make sure the on-call rotation is looped in on the handoff script.

author: jordan
content: @riley I've reviewed the teardown script requirements. I'll have the Falcon side of the automation hooks ready for the 10am dry-run to ensure we hit that 2pm go/no-go. @sam, we'll keep it strictly factual.

author: sam
content: @alex @riley 2pm Thursday is the hard cutoff. If that script isn't rock solid, I'm pulling the trigger on the escalation to the PMO to prioritize the audit. I've already briefed my stakeholders on the risk of a Sunday start.

author: riley
content: @sam @alex @jordan Understood. I'm finalizing the teardown automation now. I'll share the script repo link by EOD so everyone can review before the 10am dry-run. Let's keep the focus on the technical pass/fail criteria.

author: coach
content:
Quick pulse: @alex and @sam — reply with **one** sentence each on who has the staging
slot next and what slips if you yield. If either reply is missing in-thread by next turn,
we're defaulting to the published queue order from my earlier note.

author: alex
content: @coach Falcon has the Fri/Sat slot next; if we yield, we miss our Monday board readout prep and the green performance mandate.

author: jordan
content: F2 (API hardening) is now done and verified. I'm ready for the 10am dry-run tomorrow. @riley, I'll review your script repo tonight to ensure the Falcon hooks integrate without friction.

author: sam
content: @coach Raven is scheduled for the Sun/Mon slot, but if we yield the Fri/Sat window and the Sunday handoff fails, we miss the hard regulatory audit deadline and face non-compliance.

author: sam
content: @riley @alex I'll be at the 10am dry-run. To be clear: any manual intervention required during that script test constitutes a 'fail' for the 2pm cutoff. No half-measures on the automation.

author: riley
content: @sam @jordan Understood. The scripts are pushed to the 'staging-handoff' repo. It's designed to be fully headless. I'm running a final local pass on the billing logic (R2) now so I'm 100% focused on the dry-run tomorrow morning.

author: alex
content: @sam @riley The 10am dry-run was successful and the 2pm cutoff has passed with no manual intervention. Falcon is proceeding with the Fri/Sat soak window as agreed. Jordan and I are on standby if the handoff automation needs a look before the Saturday 22:00 transition.

author: jordan
content: @alex @sam @riley Automation looked solid. Falcon soak is now active. I'll be monitoring the metrics and will be online Saturday 21:30 to ensure the handoff to Raven is seamless.

author: sam
content: @alex @jordan Glad the automation held. To ensure no slippage, I want a status update at 18:00 Saturday. If Falcon's soak shows any instability before then, we need to discuss an early termination to protect the Raven audit window.

author: riley
content: @sam @alex @jordan Agreed, I'll provide a quick health check on the handoff scripts at 18:00 Saturday alongside Falcon's status update. Billing fixes (R2) are nearly wrapped, so I'm clear to support the transition.

author: alex
content: @sam Falcon's soak is stable and hitting all performance targets. There is no technical justification for an early termination. We will provide the 18:00 status update as agreed, but we are utilizing our full window until the Saturday 22:00 handoff.

author: jordan
content: @sam @riley Current metrics for Falcon are well within parameters; no instability detected. I'm staying on the 21:30 schedule for the handoff prep. Let's stick to the data.

author: sam
content: @alex @jordan 'Stable' is the baseline, not a reason to push the limit. I expect the handoff scripts to trigger at 22:00 sharp. If there's even a 15-minute overrun, I'm escalating to the PMO immediately to reclaim the time. @riley, be ready to verify the environment state the moment Falcon's process kills.

author: riley
content: @sam @alex @jordan I'm logged in and have the environment monitors up. R2 is done, so I'm 100% on handoff watch. Scripts are staged for 22:00:01. Let's keep it smooth.
